content:
# Machine Learning Geoscience

**Summary**: The use of machine learning algorithms to predict geological properties from geophysical data.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015]] implemented Radial Basis Function (RBF) Neural Networks to predict lithology and porosity.

- [[2020-abdallah|Abdallah, 2020]] applied machine learning techniques (ANN) to automate the identification of suitable CO2 storage sites.

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] evaluated machine learning algorithms like DBSCAN for automated first arrival picking.

- [[2021-shaheen|Shaheen, 2021]] employed machine-learning-geoscience via a CNN to improve the detection of low-magnitude events.

- [[2022-awais|Awais, 2022]] utilized unsupervised machine learning (DBSCAN) as a more flexible alternative to supervised CNN methods.

- [[2023-suleymanli|Suleymanli, 2023]] utilized machine learning to develop a resolution-invariant model for real-time monitoring.

- [[2023-almarzooq|Almarzooq, 2023]] applied machine-learning-geoscience to automate the inversion of VSP data.

- [[2025-ferreira|Ferreira, 2025]] explored the role of machine learning in petrography, remote sensing, and seismic processing.

## Instruments and Software



- [[2020-abdallah|Abdallah, 2020]] applied machine learning techniques (ANN) to automate the identification of suitable CO2 storage sites.

- [[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021]] evaluated machine learning algorithms like DBSCAN for automated first arrival picking.

- [[2021-shaheen|Shaheen, 2021]] employed machine-learning-geoscience via a CNN to improve the detection of low-magnitude events.

- [[2022-awais|Awais, 2022]] utilized unsupervised machine learning (DBSCAN) as a more flexible alternative to supervised CNN methods.

- [[2023-suleymanli|Suleymanli, 2023]] utilized machine learning to develop a resolution-invariant model for real-time monitoring.

- [[2023-almarzooq|Almarzooq, 2023]] applied machine-learning-geoscience to automate the inversion of VSP data.

- [[2025-ferreira|Ferreira, 2025]] explored the role of machine learning in petrography, remote sensing, and seismic processing.

## Typical Workflow



## Key Results and Findings

- RBF neural networks showed improved correlation and lower error compared to multi-linear regression. ([[2015-fuad|Fuad, 2015, p. 64]])

- Used ANN to predict permeability and classify lithology with high correlation coefficients. ([[2020-abdallah|Abdallah, 2020, p. 70]])

- DBSCAN is preferred in most cases because it does not require a training data set compared to supervised methods. ([[2021-almashhor|AlMashhor, 2021, p. 34]])

- The CNN achieved 100% accuracy on training/validation data and outperformed traditional methods like STA/LTA. ([[2021-shaheen|Shaheen, 2021, p. 41]])

- Unsupervised methods do not require labeled data, making them more flexible for various geophysical tasks. ([[2022-awais|Awais, 2022, p. 36]])

- Machine learning offers potential for detecting, locating, and interpreting seismic events using various architectures like FNOs. ([[2023-suleymanli|Suleymanli, 2023, p. 21]])

- Consecutive neural networks provided a 10x speedup over traditional and other tested models. ([[2023-almarzooq|Almarzooq, 2023, p. 16]])

- Machine learning has seen exponential growth in the last decade for tasks like image classification and object detection. ([[2025-ferreira|Ferreira, 2025, p. 17]])
